description: In Western Australia, learner and novice drivers must maintain zero BAC while driving.
During wet weather, reduce speed and increase following distance because roads become slippery and stopping distances increase.
Under WA's SLOMO law, you must slow to 40 km/h and move over safely when passing stationary emergency or breakdown vehicles displaying flashing lights.
Do not drive if you are affected by fatigue, illness, alcohol, drugs or medication that could impair safe driving.
Good drivers continuously scan ahead and around the road environment to identify and respond to hazards early.
When driving near trucks or buses, leave extra space and avoid remaining in blind spots for long periods.
Trailers and loads must remain within legal towing and weight limits and be secured safely before travel.
A NO U-TURN sign means making a U-turn at that location is prohibited.
You must not park too close to intersections, traffic lights or pedestrian crossings unless signs permit it.
Drivers must give way to pedestrians at crossings and when turning into a road pedestrians are crossing.
Police may require drivers to undergo breath or drug testing, and refusing a lawful test can result in penalties.
In Western Australia, school zones commonly operate at 40 km/h during signed school times.
At a stop sign or stop line, you must stop completely and give way before entering the intersection.
In wet conditions, leave a larger gap behind other vehicles to allow for reduced traction and longer braking distances.
Avoid driving too closely behind trucks because your visibility and reaction time may be reduced.
Obey temporary speed limits and directions in roadwork areas to protect workers and other road users.
Bus lanes may only be used by permitted vehicles unless signs allow other vehicles to enter temporarily.
Loading zones are restricted to vehicles permitted by the sign and may not be used for general parking.
